scenarios.description: clone() on empty Document produces a distinct deep copy with no child nodes (loop-0 children)
scenarios.precondition: Document doc = new Document("http://example.com"); // no children
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Document != original; returned.childNodes.size() == 0; returned.baseUri() equals original.baseUri(); returned.outputSettings != original.outputSettings; returned.parser != original.parser

scenarios.description: clone() on Document with multiple children produces deep copies of all children (loop-N children)
scenarios.precondition: Document doc = Document.createShell("http://test"); // has html, head, body children
scenarios.expectedResult: returned.childNodes().size() == 4; for each index i, returned.childNodes().get(i) != original.childNodes().get(i); returned.childNodes().get(i).outerHtml() equals original.childNodes().get(i).outerHtml()

scenarios.description: clone() yields a separate outputSettings instance (modify clone.outputSettings, original unaffected)
scenarios.expectedResult: clone.outputSettings().prettyPrint() == false; modifying clone.outputSettings().prettyPrint(true) does not change doc.outputSettings().prettyPrint() (remains false)

scenarios.description: clone() yields a separate parser instance (modify clone.parser, original unaffected)
scenarios.expectedResult: clone.parser().getName() equals original.parser().getName() initially; setting clone.parser(Parser.htmlParser()) does not change original.parser().getName()
